objective: Evaluate whether a target-set indexed forecasting decoder can expose future targets as model inputs, control the single-model amortization gap, improve prefix consistency, and provide a stable carrier for later future-aware or MoE mechanisms.

latest_decision.issue: The target-set interface mechanically solves prefix consistency and keeps the single-model amortization gap within +1.0%, but it fails strict compatibility because ETTh2 h96 and ETTm1 h96 are slightly worse than the fixed H720-prefix reference; h96/ETTm1 remains the main accuracy weakness.
latest_decision.rollback_step: Return to Step 5-6 for a targeted repair of short-horizon capacity and H720-prefix reuse, not to MoE. The target-side state is promising but not yet a passed carrier.
latest_decision.next_direction: Repair the target-set decoder with a short-prefix specialist residual, horizon-balanced objective, or capacity-preserving local prefix path; then rerun a targeted h96/h192 compatibility gate before future-aware or MoE.

active_repair.problem: The first target-set decoder mechanically solves prefix consistency but misses strict compatibility because short-prefix capacity is slightly weaker than fixed H720-prefix on ETTh2 h96 and ETTm1 h96, and h96 remains weak versus horizon-specific specialists.
active_repair.hypothesis: A zero-initialized dense residual readout shared by the first target segments can repair h96/h192 capacity without reintroducing horizon-specific heads or breaking prefix stability.
active_repair.pass_condition: H720-aligned h96/h192 strict no-degradation passes, h96 mean relative MSE improves over +2.74%, overall mean relative MSE stays <= +0.62%, and prefix mismatch remains near zero.

result.decision: failed; the dense prefix residual became an uncontrolled correction and worsened short-prefix capacity instead of repairing it

active_next_candidate.problem: The first target-set decoder preserves prefix consistency by using independent target queries, but this likely under-models future segment dependency that horizon-specific fixed heads can encode implicitly.
active_next_candidate.existence_evidence: PatchEncoderTargetSetDecoder reaches numerical prefix consistency but remains +0.62% mean relative MSE vs specialists; PatchEncoderTargetSetPrefixResidual worsens to +2.03% and raises target-state cosine to 0.836250, so uncontrolled output residual capacity is not the right repair.
active_next_candidate.hypothesis: Causal target self-attention over target states can model future segment dependency while preserving prefix stability, because segment j only attends to target states <= j.
active_next_candidate.pass_condition: Mean relative MSE is no worse than +0.62%, h96 mean relative MSE improves below +2.74%, H720-prefix h96/h192 comparison does not regress, prefix mismatch remains near zero, and target states do not collapse toward the prefix residual cosine of 0.836250.

local_smoke.decision: passed; causal target interaction path writes required artifacts and preserves prefix consistency at numerical zero level

active_candidate.problem: Horizon-specific direct forecasting treats the target horizon as an external training-script setting rather than a model input, creating separate specialist heads that do not unify target positions, prefix consistency, or future-step dependency.
active_candidate.hypothesis: A target-set indexed decoder can expose requested future positions as model inputs, reduce prefix inconsistency, and provide a stable target-side state carrier for later future-aware or MoE mechanisms while keeping the single-model amortization gap controlled.
active_candidate.pass_condition: Compatibility pass: one mixed target-set model has mean relative MSE <= +1.0% versus horizon-specific PatchEncoderFixedHead, no dataset average degradation worse than +3.0%, improves h96/h192 versus H720-prefix reuse, reduces prefix mismatch, and produces non-degenerate target states. Paper-core pass requires mean relative MSE < 0 versus specialists or follow-up mechanisms converting the target-side state into stable forecast gains.

decision_boundary.phase1a: one-to-one horizon training; completed with no passing fixed-head patch candidate
decision_boundary.phase1r: mixed target-set training; tests single-model target-position interface, amortization gap, prefix consistency, and target-state carrier quality
decision_boundary.phase1b: one-model compatibility and follow-up mechanisms; run only if Phase1-R reaches at least compatibility pass

PatchEncoderTargetSetDecoder.target_interface: future segment queries are model inputs and condition a dense history readout
PatchEncoderTargetSetDecoder.readout_policy: preserve dense access to flattened history states to avoid the SegmentQueryHead capacity collapse

PatchEncoderTargetSetPrefixResidual.repair_scope: shared dense residual readout for early target segments only

PatchEncoderCausalTargetInteraction.repair_scope: model target-segment dependency without allowing later target queries to alter earlier prefixes

remote.gpu_policy: check nvidia-smi before every run; prefer GPU 1 or 2 when memory is safe; GPU_IDS may contain one or two selected GPUs
